title: Quiz: what a UNIQUE constraint prevents
prompt_md: You add a `UNIQUE` constraint to the `users.email` column. What does it prevent?
options: It prevents the email column from ever being updated
It prevents two different rows from storing the same email value
It prevents the email value from being null
It prevents the table from having more than one user total
explanation_md: A `UNIQUE` constraint guarantees no two rows share the same value in that column — so the database rejects an insert or update that would create a duplicate email. It doesn't block updates in general, and uniqueness is separate from nullability (`NOT NULL` handles nulls; a unique column can still allow one null). It limits duplicate values, not the total number of rows.
